figure.title: Segment Packing Algorithm
figure.purpose: Describes the logic flow for packing Transaction Layer (TL) Flits and Alternate (ALT) sectors into a Data Link segment.

technical_summary: The process starts with optionally adding a DL ALT sector.
It then checks for 'Carry over' data from a previous TL Flit and adds it if present.
It attempts to add a new TL Flit (TL Flit[0]). If the segment runs out of space, it zero-fills the remainder or saves the rest as 'carry over' for the next segment.
If space remains (unallocated sector), it attempts to add a second TL Flit (TL Flit[1]) using similar logic.

steps: Add DL ALT sector if needed.
Add any carry over from a previous flit.
Pack TL Flit[0]. If it doesn't fit completely, generate carry over.
If space remains, pack TL Flit[1]. If it doesn't fit completely, generate carry over.
Zero-fill unused space if a flit cannot be fully packed or there is no more data.

critical_facts: TL Flits can span across segment boundaries, requiring a 'carry over' mechanism to preserve the remaining flit data for the subsequent segment.
